threats.title: Credential stuffing, weak policy, or missing MFA
threats.description: Reused or guessable passwords with no second factor let an attacker authenticate as the user.

threats.title: Token forgery via weak signing or algorithm confusion
threats.description: A weak secret or an accepted 'none'/HS-vs-RS confusion lets an attacker mint tokens.

threats.title: Auth events and lockouts are not logged
threats.description: Failed logins, resets, and lockouts leave no record, so account takeover attempts are invisible.

threats.title: User enumeration through differing responses
threats.description: Login or reset responses differ for known vs unknown accounts, revealing which users exist.

threats.title: No lockout or rate limit enables brute force
threats.description: Unthrottled login lets an attacker brute-force credentials and, incidentally, lock out real users.

threats.title: Privilege escalation via role tampering
threats.description: A client-supplied role/claim is trusted, or a missing check lets a user assume a higher role.
